description_long: This wheelchair features a robust, tubular metal frame finished in a deep red color, with large spoked rear wheels and smaller front caster wheels for maneuverability. The seat and backrest are constructed from a black, padded material, likely vinyl or synthetic leather, providing comfort and support. The armrests are solid and extend the full length of the seat, while the footrests are dual, flat, and positioned at the front, designed to support both feet comfortably. The rear wheels are equipped with push rims, allowing the user to self-propel the chair. The frame includes cross-bracing beneath the seat, indicating a folding design for portability and storage. The push handles at the back are standard, straight, and capped with rubber grips for ease of use by an attendant.
description: A red metal-framed wheelchair with large spoked rear wheels, black padded seat and backrest, dual footrests, and standard push handles. The design includes cross-bracing for folding and push rims for self-propulsion.
description_view_0: Side view showing the red tubular frame, large spoked rear wheel with push rim, dual footrests, and padded seat and backrest.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the dual footrests, wide padded seat, and the symmetrical armrests.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, with clear visibility of the frame, rear wheel, and footrest configuration.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the backrest, standard push handles, and the alignment of the large rear wheels.
attribute_wheel_type: Spoked rear wheels (2 large), solid rubber front caster wheels (2 small); rear wheels have push rims and thin tires.
attribute_frame_design: Folding frame with tubular steel construction and cross-bracing under the seat for structural support.
attribute_footrest_style: Dual fixed footrests, flat and rectangular, positioned side by side at the front.
attribute_handlebar_configuration: Standard straight push handles with rubber grips, extending from the top rear of the backrest.
